data.title: German Foreign Minister Optimistic About U.S.-Europe Compromise on Greenland Issue
data.description: According to Odaily, German Foreign Minister Johann Wadeful expressed optimism about Europe's ability to reach a compromise with the United States regarding the Greenland issue. After meeting with U.S. Secretary of State Rubio in Washington on Monday, Wadeful stated his hope for U.S. involvement in a new NATO mission in the Arctic region to help ensure Greenland's security. Wadeful remarked, "NATO is currently beginning to formulate specific plans, which will be discussed with our American partners. Although we have not completed this step today, all parties are prepared to work within the NATO framework. Germany will also strive to contribute to this effort." Wadeful also discussed ongoing negotiations between the U.S., Europe, and Ukraine with Rubio. Germany has clearly indicated its willingness to support a military mission as part of security assurances.

data.description: White House Press Secretary Levitt: Airstrikes against Iran are one of many options.

data.title: Bessant warns: The Fed investigation could trigger market turmoil and lead Powell to refuse to relinquish power.
data.description: According to AXIOS, two sources familiar with the matter revealed that U.S. Treasury Secretary Bessant told Trump Sunday evening that the federal investigation into the Federal Reserve Chairman had "made a mess" and could have adverse effects on financial markets. One source familiar with the call between Bessant and Trump said, "The secretary was very unhappy, and he's let the president know about it." Trump appears to be deliberately distancing himself from the investigation into Fed Chairman Powell. The source said that the Washington office of U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro launched the investigation without prior notification to the Treasury Department, senior White House officials, or the Justice Department. Another source said that Bessant "thought that Powell would leave when the president appointed a new Fed chairman. But that's not happening now. Now Powell is taking a tougher stance. It's really made a mess of things." However, the source said that Bessant did not question the necessity of investigating Powell in his conversation with Trump, nor was he defending the Fed chairman.

data.title: Trump: Any country doing business with Iran will be subject to a 25% tariff.
data.description: US President Trump: Effective immediately, any country doing business with Iran will be subject to a 25% tariff on all its commercial activities with the United States. This order is final and binding.

data.title: U.S. Small-Cap Stocks Achieve Longest Winning Streak in Seven Years
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, U.S. small-cap stocks have reached a significant milestone, marking their longest winning streak against large-cap stocks in seven years. On Monday, the Russell 2000 Index closed at a record high, extending its consecutive victories over the S&P 500 Index to seven days. The last time a longer winning streak occurred was in January 2019, when the stock market was recovering from a sharp decline.
